## Purpose
This file serves as the configuration for the Node.js project named "diffdoc," which translates repository code shifts into plain-English business context.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users can run commands such as `npm start` to build and execute the application, or `npm run clean` to remove the `dist` directory. The project can be installed and used as a command-line tool with the `diffdoc` and `diffdoc-mcp` commands.

## Business Rules
The project is licensed under the MIT license, requires Node.js version 22 or higher, and has specific build and clean scripts defined. The dependencies and devDependencies are managed according to their specified versions.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
The file specifies input data in the form of dependencies and scripts, and outputs the built application in the `dist` directory. It also includes metadata such as the project name, version, and author information.

## Side Effects
Running the `clean` script deletes the `dist` directory, and the `build` script compiles TypeScript files into JavaScript, generating output files in the `dist` directory.

## Error And Edge Cases
If the required Node.js version is not met, the project may fail to run. Additionally, if the `dist` directory does not exist when running the `clean` script, it may result in an error unless handled by the `force` option.

## Dependencies
The project relies on several external packages, including `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk`, `commander`, `ignore`, `openai`, `simple-git`, `vectra`, and `zod`. It also uses `@types/node` and `typescript` as development dependencies.

## Operational Notes
The project is designed to be built using TypeScript, and the build process is initiated through the `npm run build` command. It is important to ensure that the Node.js environment meets the specified version requirement for proper functionality.
